role: Chair of the Reform Front of Iran (July 2023–present); secretary-general of the Union of Islamic Iran People Party
bio:
Azar Mansouri is an Iranian reformist politician and women's rights activist, best known as the first woman to chair a major Iranian political coalition. In July 2023 she succeeded [Behzad Nabavi](/people/behzad-nabavi) as chair of the [Reform Front of Iran](/orgs/reform-front-of-iran), the main umbrella coalition of Iran's reformist political camp.

She is a founding member and secretary-general of the Union of Islamic Iran People Party, and a senior member of the [Islamic Iran Participation Front](/orgs/islamic-iran-participation-front). A pioneer of Iranian civil society organizing, she was a leading campaigner for the One Million Signatures initiative for women's rights. She was disqualified from the 2008 parliamentary election by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) and arrested following the disputed 2009 presidential election that triggered the [Green Movement](/events/green-movement-protests-2009).

On 8 February 2026, Iranian authorities arrested Mansouri along with two other reformist figures — Ebrahim Asgharzadeh and Mohsen Aminzadeh — on charges of 'targeting national unity, taking a stance against the constitution, coordination with enemy propaganda, promoting surrender, diverting political groups and creating secret subversive mechanisms.' She was released days later, though the charges remained pending.

sources.title: Iran arrests three reformist political figures

editorial_notes: Born c. 1964, Varamin. First woman to chair the Reform Front. Secretary-general of UIIPP. Arrested February 2026, released days later.
